## Summary

Implement a **credit event dispute flow** so users can flag incorrectly reported events and operators can review — critical for trust in partner-reported scores (#17–#23).

---

## Background

Partners report events via `POST /api/events/report`. If a platform reports a fraudulent or incorrect event:
- User score is inflated/deflated immediately
- No self-service correction path exists
- txHash uniqueness prevents duplicate reports but not wrong ones

---

## Proposed implementation

### Data model

```prisma
model CreditEventDispute {
  id            String   @id @default(uuid())
  creditEventId String
  walletAddress String
  reason        String
  status        DisputeStatus @default(PENDING)
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  resolvedAt    DateTime?
  resolution    String?
}

enum DisputeStatus {
  PENDING
  UNDER_REVIEW
  ACCEPTED
  REJECTED
}
```

### User API

`POST /api/user/{wallet}/disputes`
```json
{ "creditEventId": "...", "reason": "I did not complete this escrow" }
```

Requires wallet auth (#35).

### Admin API

`PATCH /api/admin/disputes/{id}` — accept (reverse score impact) or reject

### Score reversal on accept

Recalculate user score from remaining events + stellar base (see #56 recalc script logic)

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] User can submit dispute for their own credit events only
- [ ] Duplicate dispute on same event returns 409
- [ ] Admin accept reverses score impact and marks event `disputed: true`
- [ ] Dispute status visible in dapp history page
- [ ] Audit log entry on resolution (#43 admin dashboard)
- [ ] Swagger documented

---

## Related

- #43 admin dashboard
- #34 score history
- #17–#23 partner integrations

---

## Out of scope

- On-chain dispute resolution
- Automated fraud ML detection
